And so we need to continue to invest in sort of more scalable reach.
And so one of the ways that we're focused on doing that is by getting stronger in both traditional SEO and then trying to get ahead of the curve to a certain extent in AEO.
We're also a small and sort of scrappy team for our size, I would say.
We're growing, but, you know, we need to really lean into AI to scale our ability to produce enough content in sort of all of the corners that our buyers are looking for.
Things like us are looking for us and meet them where they are.

And then the third is really helping them differentiate their customer experience, more personalized, more proactive, they have a deeper understanding of the voice of the customer across every single conversation that they're having and distilling that information into.

I'd say like the reason I keep saying traditionally where we've played is the AI agent product in motion does sort of expand upon that ICP to a certain extent where like we don't want to limit ourselves anymore to just opportunities where we think about getting into.

A contact center replacing contact center employees, like there's a lot more use cases that sort of extend beyond that to like, you know, web concierge type things, 24-hour support, proactive outbound in ways that they haven't been doing anymore.

So I think like in the past, we've been very anchored on the number of people employed within a contact center and the volume of interactions that the company is doing, whereas now there are opportunities that sort of extend into the green fields of things that companies aren't doing or, you know, up and coming rapid growth companies that haven't had that contact center, that human capital in place before, but are their businesses growing in a way where they would have traditionally needed to scale there, but are now thinking like, are we going to do that?
Are we going to do this with AI agents?
Are we going to do that with some combination?

I'm like, yeah.

The much more likely scenarios is that contact center leaders are being charged to do more with less.

And so they're looking for ways to get more out of their existing team and lean into the AI transformation in the future, which is AI handling a lot of those customer-facing interactions that don't need the human touch, which we still think they're
Whereas in the future, it's going to be just as critical for the human touch to exist.
But AI certainly will replace some of those easier, especially more transactional conversations.
So that's one.
The second one is, and this gets a little bit into who within that ICP we actually work with.

But digital leaders are looking to better incorporate customer lifecycle, call it information, data, background, into the customer experience across digital channels, especially the website and chat and app.
So they look to Cresta for that.
Another trigger event is they spend some degree of hundreds of hours a month digging through individual customer faces.
And conversations to extract voice of customer insights that they're like, we're not moving fast enough.
And we adapt our business processes according to this insight and information.
United Airlines, as an example, used to spend a couple hundred months or a hundred hours a month trying to extract information around how people were reacting to their rollout of basic economy.
or what their complaints were before they even were boarding the plane.
And it was because of Cresta that they actually introduced new functionality, like their new waitlist beta, as an example.
So those are a few of the main trigger points that we see.
This is helpful.
And then, and then just so I'm clear, um, like the do more with less is essentially, uh, or the do more with less is a different trigger than, uh,
My company is growing, and I need to be able to handle that increased demand.
Are you seeing both, or is it more so I need to be more operationally efficient and save money without hiring more people?
It's definitely both of those, both under the guise of do more with less.
I was talking to a customer last week, and they're like, we are on track.
They're a healthcare company.
And they're like, we're probably going to 2.5x our estimations for customers this year.
And we only found that out on October 1st, because that's when everything happens.
And so we've got to move quickly.

so they have like a do more with less type of scenario, where they're looking to augment their entire workforce with AI, because they just can't, I mean, they don't have time not going to hire another 150 agents or something like that.
So yeah, it's definitely both of those.
We're like growing.
Really fast where we're looking to get leaner.
There's even like an intra-year angle on some of that as well, right?

Like company, you'll see in like the verticals that we just talked through, a lot of them have like sort of major seasonal catalysts where like they have a huge demand spike, you know, whether that's open enrollment in the healthcare and insurance space, whether that is like the busy seasons in travel and hospitality and all the holidays and retail and stuff.
So you even get some of that like rapid surge and contraction in terms of like the workforce needed or ability to handle volume needed within a given year.
And a lot of the things we can do can help companies manage that a lot more effectively.

That's big.
Revenue leaders, so companies that own revenue collected or leaders that own revenue overall or are focused on sort of high volume sales, both inbound and outbound.
You know, we have a lot of those types of personas.

Increasingly, like CIOs and CTOs as well, sort of, as Russell mentioned, have that CEO or board level mandate and are looking for sort of like the most proven areas to actually go and apply AI and scale it.

you.

Other areas are much more greenfield.

not yet done it seriously, and it's completely greenfield.

Like there's been IVRs and virtual agent platforms and chatbots and voicebots for, you know, going on 30 years now.
But there's just this like significant technology paradigm shift that happened in the last two years.

So like the way we do it is typically entirely greenfield, but is really, really, as I'm sure I don't have to tell you, really hot right now in terms of companies trying to explore that and assess its viability and players in the market.
So highly competitive, but not typically displacing a like-for-like solution that's already in place.

might have an older, this is very prevalent in financial services and healthcare in particular.

through into my actual application, as an example.

We're building either for our customers in perpetuity or building the first major master agent and allowing them to make some tweaks to sub-agents.

As an example, we're you Thank

We're still building integrations with TalkDesk as a CCAS platform.
We're still building integrations with Nextiva as a UCAS platform, which is like, how do we ingest conversations and then plug into those things that we don't necessarily have?

mean, that's probably the one I see the most as like a common scenario we have to overcome is most of the things we do in some surface level capacity are offered to the majority of our customers and prospects by some other vendor that they already work with.
Typically, a bigger company that doesn't specialize in that.
So that's their CRM provider.
That's their contact center as a service provider.

Increasingly, they're heavily marketing their own AI agents or, you know, re-skinned deterministic chatbots and voicebots.
And we have a deeper, more mature product than all of them in all of those areas.
But we're also more expensive.

so the justification of bringing on a new vendor and showing that, like, incremental value and additional depth and expertise and why it's going to be so much better than just bundling what Genesis is telling me is exactly the same, the vendor that I've worked with for 20-plus years, is a common thing we have to overcome.
And then I'd say in sort of like each, the beginning of each major hype cycle of the products that we
We've run into pretty significant desire from sort of more tech-forward teams to build the thing in-house.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Kickoff for the Cresta \<\> GrowthX content partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=554.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Goal:** Build a scalable content engine for traditional SEO and AEO to support Cresta's triple-digit growth and market expansion.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=586.0)
  - [**Focus:** Content for broad discoverability (e.g., competitive comparisons, glossaries), not deep technical product content, which remains an internal function.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2851.0)
  - [**Value Prop:** Cresta's unique value is its deep partnership model (forward-deployed PMs/engineers) and end-to-end platform, not just the business outcomes it delivers.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=1000.0)
  - [**Blockers:** Key sales objections include Cresta's higher price vs. bundled solutions, a "build vs. buy" mindset for AI agents, and missing out-of-the-box integrations.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2267.0)

## Topics

### Why Now? The Need for Scalable Content

  - [**Company Growth:** Cresta is growing triple-digits annually and expanding from large enterprise (Fortune 500/100) into mid-market (500+ employees) and new regions (APAC, EMEA).](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=586.0)
  - [**Channel Shift:** AEO (AI Engine Optimization) is a critical new channel, already driving 8–20% of pipeline from ChatGPT, but without a formal strategy or measurement.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=692.0)
  - [**Team Bottleneck:** The small marketing team lacks the capacity and specialized expertise (SEO/AEO) to produce content at the required scale.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=3081.0)

### Cresta's Value Proposition

  - [**Business Outcomes:**](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=886.0)
      - [**Grow Revenue:** Improve conversion in sales and service-to-sales motions.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=886.0)
      - [**Save Money:** Increase operational efficiency via agent assist and AI automation.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=911.0)
      - [**Differentiate CX:** Enable personalized, proactive experiences and better VoC insights.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=942.0)
  - [**Customer Perception (The "Why Cresta"):**](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=1000.0)
      - [**Exceptional Partnership:** Deeply understands customer KPIs and provides forward-deployed PMs/engineers to ensure measurable ROI.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=1000.0)
      - [**End-to-End Platform:** A single partner for the entire AI CX journey, from agent augmentation to full automation.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=1072.0)

### Customer Profile & Trigger Moments

  - [**Ideal Customer Profile (ICP):**](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=1515.0)
      - [**Verticals:** Financial Services, Healthcare (payer), Telco, Travel, Retail, Home Services.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=1515.0)
      - [**Size:** Historically 5k+ employees, now expanding to 250+ employees.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=1640.0)
      - [**Use Cases:** High-volume contact centers for sales, customer care, service-to-sale, churn retention, and collections.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=1575.0)
  - [**Trigger Moments:**](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=1762.0)
      - [**"Do More with Less":** A primary driver, covering both cost-cutting and managing rapid growth without hiring.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=1971.0)
      - [**Executive Mandate:** CIOs/CEOs are tasked with implementing AI and seek proven solutions.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=1762.0)
      - [**Digital Transformation:** Leaders need to integrate customer data across digital channels.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=1877.0)
      - [**VoC Bottleneck:** Manual analysis of conversations is too slow to drive business decisions.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=1905.0)

### Sales Objections & Competitive Landscape

  - [**Primary Objections:**](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2267.0)
      - [**Price:** Cresta is more expensive than bundled, less-specialized solutions from incumbents (e.g., Genesys).](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2500.0)
      - [**"Build vs. Buy":** Prospects question the value of a vendor vs. building an AI agent in-house.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2572.0)
      - [**Integrations:** Missing out-of-the-box support for platforms like Zendesk Chat, TalkDesk, and Nextiva.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2439.0)
  - [**Competitive Landscape:**](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2331.0)
      - [**Incumbents:** Genesys, Nice, Variant, Nexidia offer bundled, less mature features.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2166.0)
      - [**AI Agent Specialists:** Sierra, Decagon.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2378.0)
      - [**Dev Tools:** Bland, Vapi (Cresta is not a self-service SDK).](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2390.0)

### Content Strategy & Process

  - [**Current Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2646.0)
      - [Content is driven by a 6–8 week product release schedule.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2670.0)
      - [Hyper-technical content is written by Product/Engineering, with Content providing editorial polish.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2692.0)
      - [Other content (webinars, e-books) is created by the Content Lead.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2731.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Role:**](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2862.0)
      - [**Focus:** Broadly discoverable content for SEO/AEO.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2862.0)
      - [**Examples:** Competitive comparison pages, sitemap glossaries, and general educational content.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2907.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This avoids the deep product nuance that slows down external teams and leverages Cresta's internal expertise for technical content.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=2851.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=3153.0)
      - [Deliver initial artifacts (writing guidelines, personas, company context) for review by EOW 1.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=3153.0)
      - [Develop a full content strategy (TAM analysis, topic clusters, prioritized list) for review by EOW 2.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=3188.0)
      - [Set up the AI visibility tracking tool this week.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=793.0)
  - [**Cresta:**](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=3153.0)
      - [Provide feedback on all artifacts and the content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=3153.0)
      - [Share additional resources as needed to support research.](https://fathom.video/share/y2r6xTRH-4L4yR6aPLy5umuDoaXS5sZe?tab=summary&timestamp=3390.0)

action_items.description: Ask Valentina to trim 1–2 min pre-meeting chat from recording; send link to Cresta
Request AEO visibility tool setup; share signals w/ Cresta in weeks 2–3
Schedule product deep dive w/ Devon + PMM; then build feature matrix + messaging
Post kickoff summary in Slack to Russell, Devon, Shreha, Nicky, Annie
Share writing guidelines, company context, personas in Notion; request Cresta comments
Review Cresta links; request missing resources from Devon
